output.summary: Replace the intermediate .tabs-item-body wrapper with an outer wrapper div around each <details> element. The outer wrapper stretches full height on desktop and holds the background color + border-radius. The <details> element inside uses CSS position:sticky with transparent background on desktop. JavaScript sets the background color class on the active tab's outer wrapper instead of on the <details> element itself. On mobile, the wrapper is invisible and details/summary accordion behavior works normally.

fileChanges.description: Add outer wrapper div around <details>, remove intermediate .tabs-item-body wrapper, restructure classes
fileChanges.details:
1. Wrap the entire
element in a new
element. 2. OUTER WRAPPER classes: - Always: 'tabs-item-wrapper rounded-3xl transition-colors' - Desktop: 'md:flex-1 md:rounded-3xl' - The wrapper does NOT get bg-accent-lighter by default in PHP. On initial server render, the first tab should have the bg class set. Use a data attribute or conditional: if $index === 0, add 'bg-accent-lighter' (or the tabColor equivalent). Actually, since JS takes over on DOMContentLoaded, the PHP just needs the wrapper present. But for the initial flash before JS runs, the first item's wrapper should have bg-accent-lighter. However, we don't have an 'is first' flag in TabsItem.php easily. The JS will set it immediately on DOMContentLoaded, so a brief flash is acceptable. Keep wrapper classes minimal in PHP. - Final wrapper classes: 'tabs-item-wrapper md:flex-1 md:rounded-3xl transition-colors' - The tabColor data attribute should be on the wrapper: data-tab-color="{$tabColor}" so JS can read it. 3. DETAILS element classes - REMOVE from
: - Remove: 'md:hidden md:open:flex md:open:flex-col md:open:flex-1' - Remove: 'md:open:bg-accent-lighter md:border-0 md:rounded-3xl md:open:p-6' - Keep: 'tabs-item group transition-all overflow-hidden border border-charcoal rounded-3xl' - Keep: 'open:bg-accent open:border-accent' (mobile accordion styling) - Add for desktop: 'md:border-0 md:rounded-3xl md:bg-transparent md:overflow-visible' - Add for desktop visibility: 'md:hidden md:open:block' - Add for desktop sticky: 'md:open:sticky md:open:top-[calc(var(--fixed-elements-height,0px)+2rem)]' - The $tabColor conditional class stays on
for mobile (border/bg accent color on mobile open state), but on desktop the color goes on the wrapper via JS. 4. REMOVE the .tabs-item-body wrapper div entirely (line 39:
). 5. KEEP the .tabs-item-inner wrapper div but remove its sticky classes since sticky is now on
itself: - Change from: 'tabs-item-inner md:sticky md:top-[calc(var(--fixed-elements-height,0px)+2rem)]' - Change to: 'tabs-item-inner' 6. Summary classes: keep as-is, they handle mobile vs desktop styling correctly. 7. The md:open:p-6 that was on
needs to move. On desktop, the padding should be inside. The summary already has md:p-0, and content has md:p-0. The p-6 was providing overall padding to the details box on desktop when open. Since the wrapper now holds the background, the padding should be on the details when open on desktop: keep 'md:open:p-6' on
. Final PHP structure: ```php
...
```

fileChanges.description: Mirror the PHP changes in the editor: add outer wrapper div, remove .tabs-item-body, adjust classes
fileChanges.details:
1. Wrap the <details> element in a <div className="tabs-item-wrapper md:flex-1 md:rounded-3xl transition-colors">.

2. Update <details> classes to match PHP changes:
   - Remove: 'md:hidden md:open:flex md:open:flex-col md:open:flex-1 md:open:bg-accent-lighter md:border-0 md:rounded-3xl md:open:p-6'
   - Keep: 'tabs-item group transition-all overflow-hidden border border-charcoal rounded-3xl', 'open:bg-accent open:border-accent'
   - Add: 'md:border-0 md:rounded-3xl md:bg-transparent md:overflow-visible md:open:sticky md:open:top-[calc(var(--fixed-elements-height)+32px)] md:open:p-6'
   - Note: In editor, we don't use md:hidden since the editor shows content differently. Keep 'md:hidden md:open:flex md:open:flex-col' for editor behavior, or adjust as needed for the editor context.
   - Actually in the editor, the TabsCurrent context controls visibility via the `open` prop. The md:hidden/md:open:flex classes control frontend visibility. In the editor, all details are rendered but only the active one has open={true}. So these CSS classes still matter for the editor's desktop preview. Keep the md:hidden md:open:flex md:open:flex-col pattern but remove md:open:flex-1 (wrapper handles flex-1 now).

3. Remove the <div className="tabs-item-body md:flex-1"> wrapper (line 135).

4. Update .tabs-item-inner: remove sticky classes, just use 'tabs-item-inner'.

5. On desktop in editor, when a tab is active (displayOpen), add bg-accent-lighter to the wrapper div. Use the cn() utility:
   className={cn({'tabs-item-wrapper md:flex-1 md:rounded-3xl transition-colors': true, 'bg-accent-lighter': displayOpen, [tabColor]: tabColor !== '' && displayOpen})}

   This simulates what JS does on the frontend.

fileChanges.description: Update JS to work with new wrapper architecture: find wrappers, set background on wrapper, update selectors

No structural changes needed. The parent template renders $children which are the TabsItem blocks. The .tabs-panels container remains the same. However, verify that the md:gap-0 on .tabs-panels is correct since wrappers will now be direct children.
fileChanges.details:
The .tabs-panels container currently has 'flex-1 flex flex-col gap-4 md:gap-0 md:items-stretch'. This is correct:
- gap-4: mobile accordion items have 4 spacing between them
- md:gap-0: on desktop, wrappers stretch full height with no gap (they tile vertically to fill the flex container)
- md:items-stretch: wrappers stretch to fill width

No changes needed to Tabs.php.

designDecisions.decision: Use an outer wrapper div around <details> instead of an intermediate wrapper between <details> and content
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested this architecture change to fix CSS sticky positioning limitations with direct children of <details> elements

designDecisions.decision: Wrapper gets md:flex-1 to stretch full height in the flex column, details gets sticky positioning inside it
designDecisions.evidence: User specified 'outer wrapper stretches full height (flex-1) and holds the background color + border-radius' and 'details element inside is CSS position:sticky'

designDecisions.decision: JavaScript sets bg-accent-lighter on the active wrapper and removes it from inactive wrappers
designDecisions.evidence: User specified 'JavaScript sets the background color on the active tab outer wrapper (and removes it from others) when switching tabs'

designDecisions.decision: The wrapper div is rendered inside TabsItem (per-child), not in the parent Tabs template
designDecisions.evidence: User specified 'The wrapper is part of TabsItem.php/tsx (not the parent), so it is rendered per-child'

designDecisions.decision: On mobile, the wrapper is transparent/invisible with no visual impact
designDecisions.evidence: User specified 'On mobile: The wrapper is transparent/invisible. Details/summary works as normal accordion.'

designDecisions.decision: Remove the .tabs-item-body intermediate wrapper from revision 2
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested 'Remove the intermediate .tabs-item-body wrapper that was added in revision 2'
